summary: Balance Confirmation Phase 4 (public recipient response loop) — backend 15/15 GREEN, frontend public /confirm/:token + auditor Responses drawer fully verified. Tested: public GET (no-auth ctx + 404 on bad token), public POST /confirm (status flips to terminal 'confirmed', send-log row written, idempotent re-submission), terminal-status guard (pixel hit on confirmed token does NOT downgrade to 'opened'), public POST /dispute (multipart with file, empty-reason 400, oversize 8MB+ 413, status flips to 'disputed', file persisted as base64), auditor list_responses (auth-gated 401 without cookie, decision filter confirmed/disputed, ledger_name+our_balance+our_dr_cr enrichment, no _id leak, no file bytes leak), auditor download_response_attachment (auth-gated 401, content-type=application/pdf, 404 when no attachment), DELETE run cascade drops bc_responses (verified via direct DB count). Frontend: /confirm/:token mounts WITHOUT cookie/auth, Yes button renders confirm-form, No button renders dispute-form (with file input + dr/cr select), empty-reason on submit shows error, revisit a confirmed token jumps straight to thankyou-screen, invalid token shows 'Link Invalid or Expired' friendly screen (NOT blank). Auditor 'Responses' button next to Send Log opens the drawer (data-testid=bc-responses-drawer), filter dropdown (all/confirmed/disputed) works, confirmed response card shows party name, decision chip, responder identity, timestamp, our-books amount with Cr label, and note text. Found and fixed one real backend bug: /public/confirmation/{token}/dispute multipart fields (responder_name, responder_email, their_balance, their_dr_cr, reason) were declared without Form(...) so FastAPI was reading them as query params — every dispute submission failed with 400 'Please provide a reason' even when reason was set. Fix: added `from fastapi import Form` and wrapped each non-file form field with `= Form(...)`. After fix all 15 backend tests pass.

minor.issue: FIXED by testing agent: Form(...) annotations were missing on responder_name/responder_email/their_balance/their_dr_cr/reason. FastAPI without Form(...) treats those as query params, so multipart submissions silently lost the body. After fix: 200 OK with attachment correctly persisted to bc_responses.uploaded_content_b64. Main agent should keep this Form(...) pattern for any future multipart endpoints.

When LEDGERS doc has dr_cr stored separately on it, the public ctx ignores it and recomputes from sign(closing_balance). For trade_payables stored as positive Cr the recomputation still yields 'Cr' so output is correct, but the auditor enrichment elsewhere (list_responses) returns L['dr_cr'] directly — minor inconsistency between the two read paths. Not a defect for Phase 4 acceptance.

issues: Drawer is min(95vw, 920px); on the 1920px viewport it fully overlays the right half of the workbench including the entire right-side toolbar. The Send Log drawer has the same w=920px footprint. Consider 720px (matches Send Log content density of ~3-4 columns) or making it modal-overlay+ESC instead of side-sheet.

design_issues.screen: ConfirmPage error state ('Link Invalid or Expired')
issues: Subtitle uses error.message verbatim — when the load-fetch fails with the FastAPI 404 detail it shows 'This confirmation link is invalid or has expired.' (good), but if a transient backend error returns a different message it would surface here too. Wrap with a fallback: `{error.message?.includes('expired') ? error.message : 'This confirmation link is invalid or has expired.'}`.

action_items: Acknowledge the Form(...) fix on /public/confirmation/{token}/dispute (controller.py imports + signature) — keep this pattern.
Optionally cap Responses drawer width at min(95vw, 720px) for layout parity with Send Log content density, or convert to centred modal.
critical_code_review_comments: controller.py public_dispute: BEFORE FIX — Form(...) was missing so multipart fields silently dropped to defaults; FastAPI gave no warning. Pattern to enforce: any handler that takes UploadFile + scalars MUST annotate scalars with Form(default=...). Already corrected.

controller.py _record_response: replace_one with upsert=True keyed on response_token gives clean idempotency — re-submission overwrites the response doc but ledger.confirmation_status remains terminal. Good.

controller.py _record_response: 8MB cap enforced AFTER full file read into memory — for a 10MB upload we still buffer 10MB before raising 413. For a public unauthenticated endpoint this is a small DoS vector. Consider checking request.headers.get('content-length') early, OR streaming the read with a counter.

controller.py public_get_confirmation: never returns uploaded_content_b64 (projection excludes it), good defence — recipient browser only ever sees their own filename + size on revisit, never the bytes.
controller.py list_responses & download_response_attachment: both correctly call _auth() before any DB read — no information leak via 404-vs-401 ordering.

controller.py download_response_attachment: ext-based content-type fallback to application/octet-stream — safe. Filename on Content-Disposition is unsanitised; if a recipient sets a malicious filename it lands here. Consider quoting/normalising.
controller.py delete_run: bc_responses cascade via db.bc_responses.delete_many — verified empty after delete. Good.

ConfirmPage.jsx uses raw axios (NOT the http alias) — this is correct and intentional so no Authorization cookie is sent (App.js wraps the rest of the app in an interceptor). Comment in code makes intent explicit.

Landing.jsx ResponsesDrawer is co-located with Landing (now ~860 lines total). Carry-over recommendation from iteration_6/7: split into LedgerWorkbench / ResponsesDrawer / SendLogDrawer / TemplatesDrawer / AuthDrawer modules. Not blocking.

success_rate.backend: 100% (15/15 Phase 4 tests pass after Form(...) fix on /dispute)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on Phase 4 deltas — public no-auth route, Yes/No choices, dispute form mount, empty-reason error, revisit-thankyou, invalid-token error screen, Responses drawer + filter + response card all wired

test_credentials: session_token=qa_test_session_token_20260429_dev (admin user_qa_bot_fixed_01) — confirmed working. Public endpoints need NO auth — verified by using a separate requests.Session() with no cookie set.
seed_data_creation: Pytest fixture creates+cleans up TEST_BC_phase4 / TEST_BC_phase4_cascade runs against SEED_CLIENT cli_7f0b86b1ab0b. Each test consumes a different ledger index (0/1/2/3) so confirm/dispute don't collide on the same response_token. UI smoke reused existing run 1710de57-49a7-4a71-a411-817e4d49736b (Phase 3 demo run with 1 pre-existing confirmed response on A.Arun Capital).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 4 complete & GREEN. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_balance_confirmation_phase4.py (15 tests). The Form(...) fix on dispute is in controller.py line ~1110 — preserve. For Phase 5 (Confirmation Summary Report exports) and Phase 6 (full side-by-side recon UI), build on top of bc_responses collection — schema is locked: response_id/run_id/ledger_id/response_token/decision/responder_name/responder_email/their_balance/their_dr_cr/reason/note/responder_ip/user_agent/submitted_at/uploaded_filename/uploaded_size/uploaded_content_b64. The public POST /confirm signature is JSON (PublicConfirmRequest pydantic), but /dispute is multipart — DO NOT change that, the file upload requires it. Two potential follow-ups for E1: (a) early Content-Length pre-check on /dispute to avoid buffering >8MB, (b) drawer width parity at 720px.
rca of the issue: Root cause of the Form(...) bug: FastAPI, by default, infers parameter source from the type and the rest of the function signature. When an endpoint has `UploadFile = File(...)` plus other scalars without explicit annotation, the scalars are inferred as QUERY parameters, NOT as form fields — even though the request was multipart. Mitigation: always explicitly wrap non-file scalars in multipart endpoints with `= Form(default=...)`. Reproduction (pre-fix): `curl -X POST .../dispute -F reason=hi` → 400 'Please provide a reason explaining the difference.' Reproduction (post-fix): same curl → 200 OK with response_id.
